speaker: QUEEN
text: This way the King will come. This is the way

speaker: QUEEN
text: To Julius Caesar’s ill-erected tower,

speaker: QUEEN
text: To whose flint bosom my condemnèd lord

speaker: QUEEN
text: Is doomed a prisoner by proud Bolingbroke.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Here let us rest, if this rebellious earth

speaker: QUEEN
text: Have any resting for her true king’s queen.

speaker: QUEEN
text: But soft, but see—or rather do not see

speaker: QUEEN
text: My fair rose wither; yet look up, behold,

speaker: QUEEN
text: That you in pity may dissolve to dew

speaker: QUEEN
text: And wash him fresh again with true-love tears.—

speaker: QUEEN
text: Ah, thou, the model where old Troy did stand,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Thou map of honor, thou King Richard’s tomb,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And not King Richard! Thou most beauteous inn,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Why should hard-favored grief be lodged in thee

speaker: QUEEN
text: When triumph is become an alehouse guest?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Join not with grief, fair woman, do not so,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To make my end too sudden. Learn, good soul,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To think our former state a happy dream,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: From which awaked, the truth of what we are

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Shows us but this: I am sworn brother, sweet,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To grim necessity, and he and I

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Will keep a league till death. Hie thee to France

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And cloister thee in some religious house.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Our holy lives must win a new world’s crown,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Which our profane hours here have thrown down.

speaker: QUEEN
text: What, is my Richard both in shape and mind

speaker: QUEEN
text: Transformed and weakened? Hath Bolingbroke

speaker: QUEEN
text: Deposed thine intellect? Hath he been in thy heart?

speaker: QUEEN
text: The lion dying thrusteth forth his paw

speaker: QUEEN
text: And wounds the earth, if nothing else, with rage

speaker: QUEEN
text: To be o’er-powered; and wilt thou, pupil-like,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Take the correction, mildly kiss the rod,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And fawn on rage with base humility,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Which art a lion and the king of beasts?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: A king of beasts indeed. If aught but beasts,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: I had been still a happy king of men.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Good sometime queen, prepare thee hence for France.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Think I am dead and that even here thou takest,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: As from my deathbed, thy last living leave.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: In winter’s tedious nights sit by the fire

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With good old folks, and let them tell thee tales

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Of woeful ages long ago betid;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And, ere thou bid good night, to quite their griefs,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Tell thou the lamentable tale of me,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And send the hearers weeping to their beds.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Forwhy the senseless brands will sympathize

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The heavy accent of thy moving tongue,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And in compassion weep the fire out,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And some will mourn in ashes, some coal-black,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For the deposing of a rightful king.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: My lord, the mind of Bolingbroke is changed.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: You must to Pomfret, not unto the Tower.—

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And madam, there is order ta’en for you.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: With all swift speed you must away to France.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Northumberland, thou ladder wherewithal

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The mounting Bolingbroke ascends my throne,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The time shall not be many hours of age

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: More than it is ere foul sin, gathering head,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Shall break into corruption. Thou shalt think,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Though he divide the realm and give thee half,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: It is too little, helping him to all.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: He shall think that thou, which knowest the way

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To plant unrightful kings, wilt know again,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Being ne’er so little urged another way,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To pluck him headlong from the usurped throne.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The love of wicked men converts to fear,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That fear to hate, and hate turns one or both

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To worthy danger and deservèd death.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: My guilt be on my head, and there an end.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Take leave and part, for you must part forthwith.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Doubly divorced! Bad men, you violate

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: A twofold marriage—twixt my crown and me,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And then betwixt me and my married wife.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text:
To
                 
                Queen
                .
                 
              Let me unkiss the oath twixt thee and me;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And yet not so, for with a kiss ’twas made.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Part us, Northumberland, I towards the north,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Where shivering cold and sickness pines the clime;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: My wife to France, from whence set forth in pomp

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: She came adornèd hither like sweet May,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Sent back like Hallowmas or short’st of day.

speaker: QUEEN
text: And must we be divided? Must we part?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Ay, hand from hand, my love, and heart from heart.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Banish us both, and send the King with me.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: That were some love, but little policy.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Then whither he goes, thither let me go.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: So two together weeping make one woe.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Weep thou for me in France, I for thee here;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Better far off than, near, be ne’er the near.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Go, count thy way with sighs, I mine with groans.

speaker: QUEEN
text: So longest way shall have the longest moans.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Twice for one step I’ll groan, the way being short,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And piece the way out with a heavy heart.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Come, come, in wooing sorrow let’s be brief,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Since, wedding it, there is such length in grief.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: One kiss shall stop our mouths, and dumbly part.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Thus give I mine, and thus take I thy heart.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Give me mine own again. ’Twere no good part

speaker: QUEEN
text: To take on me to keep and kill thy heart.

speaker: QUEEN
text: So, now I have mine own again, begone,

speaker: QUEEN
text: That I may strive to kill it with a groan.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: We make woe wanton with this fond delay.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Once more, adieu! The rest let sorrow say.
